title: Conductor Quantum Introduces Coda, a Natural Language Interface for Quantum Computing - InfoQ
why:
- Coda is a natural language interface that allows users to run quantum programs on real quantum hardware by describing problems in plain language, which the platform translates into quantum circuits.
- The platform aims to reduce setup, orchestration, and low-level programming overhead, enabling users to interact with quantum computers without detailed knowledge of quantum programming models or device-specific constraints.
- Coda validates quantum circuit correctness and hardware compatibility before submitting jobs to quantum processors, preserving transparency through a learn mode that explains circuit construction and operation.
- At launch, Coda provides access to Rigetti’s 84-qubit quantum system and supports quantum simulation of up to 34 qubits using NVIDIA’s cuQuantum libraries and CUDA-Q, facilitating hybrid quantum-classical workflow testing.
- The system functions as an orchestration layer above existing quantum SDKs and hardware providers, allowing quantum programs to be generated and executed without requiring direct management of the software or hardware stack.
